title: Terrific Broth Medium
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6bcvp","text":"Tartoff, K.D. and Hobbs, C.A. (1987) Improved Media for Growing Plasmid and Cosmid Clones. Bethesda Research Laboratories Focus, 9, 12.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"ck3k","text":"Prepare the stock solutions and autoclave separately before combining when cooled to create TB medium. The reason for this is that Glycerol is believed to react with amines in the Tryptone/Yeast extract via the Malliard reaction, leading to the formation of compounds which ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Combine the following components for 1L TB buffer 24 g BD Bacto™ Yeast Extract Becton Dickinson (BD) Catalog #212750 20 g Bacto™ Tryptone Life Technologies VWR International (Avantor) Catalog #211705 4 mL Glycerol Make up volume to 900 mL with dH 2 O Autoclave 121 °C 00:30:00
Allow solutions to cool to 60 °C, or less then add 100 mL of 10x TB salts to 900 mL TB buffer.
Prepare 1L of 10x TB Salts (0.17 M KH2PO4, 0.72 M K2HPO4) by combining the following 23.1 g KH2PO4 125.4 g K2HPO4 Make up volume to 1 L with dH 2 O Autoclave 121 °C 00:30:00

We have found that using TB for plasmid preps consistently yields higher than using LB regardless of prep kit used, with half the volume of culture required. It is not recommended to exceed ~2 mL of saturated TB culture per prep column due to overloading the column and not yielding clean preps.
creator: Steven J Burgess
